title: Implement Read, Write, and Edit core tools with AST validation
purpose: Implement the three file tools that define Norn's differentiators: Read (with line numbers, binary detection), Write (with read-before-overwrite, AST validation, file length limits), and Edit (with read-before-edit, staged AST validation with rollback, blast radius reporting). These are the first tools to exercise the Tool trait lifecycle from N-006 with real compile-time pre-validate and post-validate phases. Write uses report semantics (commit then report errors). Edit uses gate semantics (stage in memory, rollback on AST failure). This is where medical-grade code quality starts.
task: Implement three tools in the tools module, each implementing the Tool trait from N-006. Read is straightforward: read a file, return content with line numbers, detect binary files and images. Write enforces read-before-overwrite in pre-validate (compile-time check using ToolContext to track read files), validates AST via tree-sitter in post-validate (report semantics: file written, error reported), and enforces file length limits. Edit enforces read-before-edit in pre-validate, applies string replacement, validates AST in post-validate (gate semantics: staged in memory, rollback on failure), and reports blast radius via symbol analysis. tree-sitter is required for AST parsing. Testing: Layer 1 unit tests with real files in temp directories.

requirements.title: Implement Read tool
requirements.spec: Read tool SHALL implement the Tool trait. It SHALL read a file and return its content with line numbers (cat -n format). It SHALL detect binary files and return a message instead of binary content. It SHALL support offset and limit parameters for partial reads. It SHALL detect image files by extension and return an appropriate indicator.
acceptance: ReadTool implements Tool trait with name 'read', effect ReadOnly
Input schema: { path: String, offset: Option<u64>, limit: Option<u64> }
Output includes line numbers in 'N\t<content>' format
Binary files (detected by null bytes in first 8192 bytes) return ToolOutput with is_error=false and content indicating binary file
Image files (.png, .jpg, .jpeg, .gif, .svg, .webp) return indicator with file path
offset=10, limit=5 returns lines 10-14 of a file
Reading a nonexistent file returns ToolOutput with is_error=true
Unit test: write a temp file, read it, verify line numbers in output

requirements.title: Implement read-tracking in ToolContext
requirements.spec: ToolContext (from N-006) SHALL track which file paths have been read during the session. WHEN the Read tool successfully reads a file, it SHALL register the path in the context. Write and Edit pre-validate checks query this state.
acceptance: ToolContext has a method: fn mark_file_read(&mut self, path: &Path)
ToolContext has a method: fn has_read_file(&self, path: &Path) -> bool
Read tool's on_success phase calls mark_file_read with the read path
Unit test: read a file via Read tool, verify has_read_file returns true for that path

requirements.title: Implement Write tool with read-before-overwrite
requirements.spec: Write tool SHALL implement the Tool trait. Pre-validate SHALL block writes to existing files that have not been read (checked via ToolContext.has_read_file). Writing new files (file does not exist) SHALL be allowed without a prior read. This check SHALL respect the AllowOverwrite context flag. WHEN AllowOverwrite is set, the check is skipped and a CheckOverride is recorded.
acceptance: WriteTool implements Tool trait with name 'write', effect Write
Input schema: { path: String, content: String }
Pre-validate blocks if file exists AND has_read_file is false: returns PreValidateOutcome::Block with reason
Pre-validate allows if file does not exist (new file creation)
Pre-validate allows if file exists AND has_read_file is true
WHEN AllowOverwrite flag is set, pre-validate allows even without prior read, and records CheckOverride with check='read_before_overwrite', flag=AllowOverwrite, source from FlagEntry
Unit test: attempt write to existing unread file, verify Block. Read file, retry, verify Proceed

requirements.title: Implement Write tool AST validation (report semantics)
requirements.spec: Write tool post-validate SHALL parse the written file with tree-sitter and check for syntax errors. Post-validate uses report semantics: the file IS written to disk, then errors are reported in the tool result. The post-validate phase SHALL NOT prevent the file from being written. tree-sitter language detection SHALL be based on file extension.
acceptance: WriteTool.post_validate_mode() returns PostValidateMode::Report
After writing, tree-sitter parses the file content using the language grammar matching the file extension
If tree-sitter finds ERROR nodes: PostValidateOutcome::Fail with errors listing line/column and error description
If no ERROR nodes: PostValidateOutcome::Pass
Supported extensions for v1: .rs, .py, .js, .ts, .json (focused set — additional grammars added in later briefs)
Unsupported extensions: post-validate returns Pass (no validation, not failure)
Unit test: write a .rs file with missing brace, verify Fail with line number. Write valid .rs, verify Pass

requirements.title: Implement Write tool file length enforcement
requirements.spec: Write tool post-validate SHALL check the written file's line count against a configurable limit (default 500, matching CO5). Path-specific overrides SHALL be supported (e.g., test files can be longer). Exceeding the limit SHALL be reported as a PostValidateOutcome::Fail.
acceptance: Default file length limit is 500 lines (excluding blank lines only — comment detection deferred to a later brief when tree-sitter line classification is built out)
Limit is configurable via ToolContext or WriteTool configuration
Path-specific overrides: patterns like '**/tests/**' or '**/*_test.rs' can have different limits
Exceeding the limit produces Fail with message including current count and limit
Unit test: write a 600-line file with default limit, verify Fail. Write a 400-line file, verify Pass

requirements.title: Implement Edit tool with read-before-edit
requirements.spec: Edit tool SHALL implement the Tool trait. It SHALL apply exact string replacement (old_string → new_string). Pre-validate SHALL block edits on files that have not been read. Pre-validate SHALL verify old_string appears exactly once in the file content (ambiguous match = block).
acceptance: EditTool implements Tool trait with name 'edit', effect Write
Input schema: { path: String, old_string: String, new_string: String }
Pre-validate blocks if has_read_file is false: returns Block with reason
Pre-validate blocks if old_string is not found in the file: returns Block with 'old_string not found'
Pre-validate blocks if old_string appears more than once: returns Block with 'old_string is ambiguous (N matches)'
Execute replaces the single occurrence of old_string with new_string
Unit test: create file, read it, edit with valid old_string, verify content changed

requirements.title: Implement Edit tool AST validation (gate semantics)
requirements.spec: Edit tool post-validate SHALL parse the edited content with tree-sitter. Post-validate uses gate semantics: the edit is staged in memory. IF AST validation fails, the original file is preserved — the edit is NOT committed to disk. IF AST validation passes, the edited content is committed. WHEN AllowBrokenAst flag is set, gate semantics downgrade to report semantics and a CheckOverride is recorded.
acceptance: EditTool.post_validate_mode() returns PostValidateMode::Gate
Edit is staged: old_string → new_string applied to in-memory content, NOT written to disk during execute
Post-validate parses the staged content with tree-sitter
If ERROR nodes found: PostValidateOutcome::Fail, original file content preserved on disk, tool result reports the syntax error with line/column
If no ERROR nodes: PostValidateOutcome::Pass, edited content committed to disk
WHEN AllowBrokenAst is set: edit is committed even on AST failure, CheckOverride recorded with check='ast_validation', flag=AllowBrokenAst, source from FlagEntry
Unit test: edit a valid .rs file to introduce syntax error, verify original file unchanged on disk. Edit with valid change, verify file updated

requirements.title: Implement Edit tool blast radius reporting
requirements.spec: Edit tool on-success SHALL report the blast radius of the change: which symbols were affected, approximate scope of impact. Initial implementation SHALL report the diff (lines changed) and affected functions/structs based on tree-sitter node analysis of the changed region.
acceptance: On-success reports: lines added, lines removed, lines modified
On-success identifies tree-sitter nodes (functions, structs, impls) that contain the changed region
Blast radius info is included in the ToolOutput content alongside the edit confirmation
Unit test: edit inside a function, verify the function name appears in blast radius report

requirements.title: Add tree-sitter dependencies
requirements.spec: Cargo.toml SHALL add tree-sitter and language grammar crates for supported languages.
acceptance: Cargo.toml includes tree-sitter
Cargo.toml includes tree-sitter-rust, tree-sitter-python, tree-sitter-javascript, tree-sitter-typescript, tree-sitter-json
Cargo.toml includes tempfile as dev-dependency for test file creation
cargo check -p norn succeeds

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement ApplyPatch tool — separate brief
SHALL NOT implement Search, Bash, Web, LSP, or agent tools — separate briefs
SHALL NOT implement LSP-based blast radius (C43 mentions LSP) — initial implementation uses tree-sitter node analysis only. LSP integration is a separate brief
SHALL NOT implement diagnostics integration (clippy/cargo check after edit) — separate brief
SHALL NOT implement file watching or filesystem event emission
SHALL NOT add RejectBrokenAst handling for Write — Write's default is report semantics, RejectBrokenAst upgrade is a future enhancement
verification: cargo check -p norn succeeds with zero errors
cargo test -p norn succeeds — all tool tests pass
cargo clippy -p norn -- -D warnings produces zero errors
Read tool tests with real temp files verify line numbers and binary detection
Write tool test with invalid .rs syntax verifies AST error is reported but file exists on disk (report semantics)
Edit tool test with invalid .rs syntax verifies original file is preserved (gate semantics)
Edit tool test with AllowBrokenAst flag verifies file IS written and CheckOverride is recorded
Write read-before-overwrite test verifies Block without prior read, Proceed with prior read
grep -rn 'unwrap()\|expect(' crates/norn/src/tools/{read,write,edit}.rs returns no matches outside test code
